profile.name: Parham Barazesh
profile.title: Senior Software/Data Engineer
profile.about: With over a decade of experience rooted in scientific programming, I specialize in architecting scalable, data-intensive software solutions for the Norwegian tech sector. My expertise lies at the intersection of Cloud-Native Architecture, Advanced Data Engineering, and Machine Learning. I bridge the gap between complex algorithmic challenges and production-grade software. From designing proprietary Master Data Management (MDM) platforms and Reverse ETL pipelines to engineering Computer Vision systems for infrastructure analysis, I build systems that turn raw data into strategic assets.

coreArchitecturalFocus.title: Cloud-Native Systems
items: Designing resilient microservices and distributed systems
Containerized workloads with Docker and Kubernetes

coreArchitecturalFocus.title: Data Engineering
items: High-throughput ETL and Reverse ETL pipelines
Reliable data platform architecture for analytics

coreArchitecturalFocus.title: Scientific Computing & AI
items: Model-enabled product features for real business impact
Python, C#, and database design (relational and non-relational)
Cloud development across Azure, GCP, and AWS
Web technologies: HTML, CSS, JavaScript, Bootstrap, React, Django, ASP.NET
Web APIs: Django REST Framework, Flask, FastAPI
Docker and Kubernetes for data workloads
Data visualization with Matplotlib, D3.js, and Plotly

education.detailText: I have studied in the University of Stavanger, UiS, through academic years 2017-2020 in M.Sc. of Petroleum Well Engineering with minor in Drilling Automation. I have worked on my Master thesis research in flow dynamics modelling under supervision of Dr. Tore Flåtten. I have researched and developed a scientific code to numerically solve a system of hyperbolic conservation laws (Isothermal and Full Euler model) for fluid flow in well and pipelines.
details: Thesis: Numerical modelling of temperature-dependent flow dynamics in drilling operations
Link: https://uis.brage.unit.no/uis-xmlui/handle/11250/2633835

Architected complex data integration pipelines utilizing Sesam’s proprietary delta-stream engine, optimizing for Change Data Capture (CDC) to minimize processing overhead and ensure low-latency synchronization.
Designed and implemented dynamic, graph-like data models to unify heterogeneous sources, creating a semantic layer that decoupled raw data structures from downstream analytical consumption.
Developed automated logic for data deduplication and reconciliation, ensuring a "Single Source of Truth" (SSOT) across distributed ERP and CRM landscapes.
Architected real-time ingestion topologies using Confluent Cloud (Kafka) and Azure Event Hubs to buffer high-velocity telemetry before processing, decoupling producers from consumers.
Deployed and managed Apache Airflow Directed Acyclic Graphs (DAGs) to handle complex, dependency-driven data transformations that extended beyond the native capabilities of the core platform.
Collaborated on Semarchy xDM implementations to enforce strict data governance policies and reference data management alongside the streaming hub.

Orchestrated high-volume Reverse ETL pipelines using Azure Data Factory, extracting enriched datasets from Snowflake and pushing them back into operational ERP and CRM systems to drive real-time business actions.

Engineered core components for a semantic Master Data Management based on delta-stream processing, moving beyond traditional batch ETL to enable real-time data consistency across heterogeneous ecosystems.
Developed and optimized high-throughput Python backend services, adhering to Test-Driven Development (TDD) to ensure reliability within a complex, distributed architecture.
Managed the application lifecycle on Azure Kubernetes Service (AKS) and on-premise clusters, utilizing advanced logging and diagnostics to drive stability and efficiency in production environments.
Led the architectural redesign of a multi-tenant synchronization service to resolve scalability bottlenecks caused by rapid user growth and increasing data volumes.
Designed a centralized Token Manager service to handle complex authorization flows, managing OAuth token lifecycles and refresh logic for external system integration.

Built critical support services including a Health Service for real-time tenant monitoring and a Bootstrapper to ensure automated, consistent deployment of high-level configurations across the cluster.
Refactored and unified the system’s API surface, enforcing strict design patterns to improve testability, maintainability, and seamless frontend integration.
Architected and maintained a Python-based Command Line Interface (CLI) used by consultants for configuration management, authentication, and environment execution.
Developed a modular connector architecture to facilitate rapid integration with third-party ERP/CRM systems, ensuring code reusability and protocol abstraction.
Implemented scripting automation (e.g., commit hash updaters) to eliminate manual overhead in deployment pipelines and ensure configuration parity across environments.
Engineered a real-time integration bridge between Dolittle Studio and complex M3 ERP environments, orchestrating the ingestion and normalization of high-throughput business telemetry.

Architected the frontend data layer for the Studio platform, a centralized operations console designed to abstract complex Kubernetes primitives, giving teams direct control over container lifecycles and artifact registries.
Designed the underlying data structures to transform raw ERP streams into actionable business intelligence, ensuring data consistency across distributed viewing components.
Developed an automated AIOps solution leveraging Prometheus time-series data to conduct real-time anomaly detection, moving system monitoring from reactive alerting to proactive incident mitigation.
Operationalized machine learning algorithms and statistical heuristics to establish dynamic baselines for system health, predicting potential outages and resource bottlenecks before they occurred.
Defined and maintained the alerting topology and query logic (PromQL), directly influencing system stability and minimizing Mean Time to Detect (MTTD) for critical infrastructure.
Architected and developed a decoupled, microservices-based platform using Django and JavaScript, ensuring modularity and high scalability across the stack.
Designed a production-grade deployment strategy on Azure, utilizing Docker containerization and Kubernetes orchestration to manage resources and ensure high availability, backed by Azure PostgreSQL.

Engineered an Industrial IoT (IIoT) simulation environment utilizing OPC UA protocols to generate high-fidelity time-series data, driving a Machine Learning pipeline for 24-hour look-ahead failure prediction.
Developed a modular ETL and notification engine, establishing seamless interoperability between heterogeneous data sinks (BigQuery, Synapse, MongoDB) and collaboration platforms (Slack, Teams).

experience.detailText: Intellectual Labs is a Norwegian tech company working on developing AI solutions to the industrial projects. We have the ambition to co-create with our partners and not to be ordinary consultants. IL takes lead on the AI development in your next generation product, in a strong collaborative partnership.
details: Architected a scalable spatio-temporal analytics engine processing high-volume cellular telemetry and geospatial topologies to model population dynamics and mobility trends.
Engineered an end-to-end Computer Vision pipeline utilizing Deep Learning (CNN, Keras, TensorFlow) on aerial drone imagery for automated road infrastructure auditing and pavement distress detection.
Designed the architecture for an NLP-driven Decision Support System, implementing semantic analysis and recommendation algorithms to deliver contextual market intelligence to enterprise users.
Developed a .NET web app to manage customer data from the Elhub web service.
oriented programming the backend modules for PWP software.
Develop the prediction and optimization of the rate of penetration (ROP) and hole cleaning efficiency modules with AI and nonlinear programming from the drilling data stream.
Testing
Sensor implementation and design of the Microcontroller (Arduino Uno and Due).
Real-time modelling of the drill string vibration from sensor data.
Develop a standalone application to animate the vibration.
Taught programming and numerical modeling courses to undergraduate students.
Designed and implemented course materials and assignments focused on practical applications of programming in engineering contexts.
Supervised student projects involving the development of computational models.
